all_fields.value:
Christ Episcopal Church in Cody, Wyoming, seeks a full-time rector. Cody is a community of about 10,400 people nestled near Yellowstone National Park's East Entrance.  We are a congregation with deep roots in our community, a proud tradition of service and worship, and a genuine hunger to deepen our faith. 

The town is large enough to provide essential services, including Cody Regional Health (a full-service hospital that attracts top medical professionals), three elementary schools, a middle school, a high school, and an alternative high school. Cody also supports a mix of locally owned shops, restaurants, and small businesses alongside larger retailers. Yellowstone Regional Airport is located in Cody and serves all of northwest Wyoming. Cody has a vibrant cultural community including several museums, public library, recreation center with an indoor ice rink, local community theaters, as well as a variety of lively music venues. The community is friendly, safe, and family-oriented. Billings, Montana, is 90 minutes away, offering expanded medical and cultural resources and additional air service. Cody is known for its Western heritage, welcoming atmosphere, and quality of life. Our parish reflects that character: independent, resilient, and deeply connected to the land and to each other. 

We seek a rector who will walk alongside us, challenge us, and guide us toward renewed spiritual growth. We yearn for worship that challenges us, leadership that models discipleship, and guidance in taking the next steps in our walk with God. We offer a community full of warmth and service, and we are eager for a leader who will help us embrace our call to love God and neighbor more fully.

Candidates wishing to apply for the position, please send the following directly to the Search Committee at Christ Church Cody:
A cover letter addressed to the Vestry describing what appeals to you about Christ Church Cody and how you see your gifts and skills matching with theirs.
Vocation Hub profile (in pdf format)
Resume

searchcommittee@christchurchcody.org

For other questions please contact Diocesan Transition Minister Rev. Canon Bobbe Fitzhugh - bobbe@episcopalwy.org

The Christ Church Budget for calendar year 2026 is $633,573. Revenues to fund this budget come from the following categories: Giving, Restricted Endowment Funds, Unrestricted Endowment Funds, Funds generated by Bargain Box Stores, Building Endowment Fund, and Grant Funds. Expenses match revenue, with operating expenses equal to $443,573, and other expenses to include disbursements for mission, grants, capitol projects, and emergency assistance. 

Christ Church has an unrestricted endowment fund of 2,920,000 and restricted endowment funds of $921,000. With the passing of major donors during recent years and the resultant
decrease in giving, the percentage of funds required from the unrestricted endowment to fulfill the budget has grown. This year’s budget requires a 7% draw from the endowment based on a
12 quarter rolling average of the unrestricted fund.

The monies generated by the operations of the two Bargain Box Stores is used exclusively for mission outreach to the community. This year’s budget anticipates a sprinkler system upgrade
funded from the endowment restricted building fund and the creation of a prayer garden which will be funded from a grant from the Foundation for the Episcopal Diocese of Wyoming.

Mileage to be paid at the IRS rate for mileage reimbursement.

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value:
Future leaders of our Episcopal worshipping community must embody a vibrant blend of strong authentic faith, which they draw upon in everyday life.  We are looking for a dynamic leader with faith to guide, model and teach us how to grow in love of God and neighbor.  Ideally, this would be someone who is charismatic and enthusiastic in the pulpit--able to inspire with both word and presence.  He/she would be a true shepherd, drawing people in, sparking renewed interest and commitment  to life in the church.  More specifically,  nurturing our youth programs to ignite young spirits as well as exploring intergenerational worship for life-long learning.  We hope for an individual gifted in pastoral counseling with a commitment to developing deep relationships with parishioners.

We envision someone who builds community with warmth, authenticity and strategic planning, helping individuals discover their unique gifts and guiding them to grow in service and spirit.  Their passion will radiate beyond our walls by developing relationships within the broader Cody community.  Most importantly, we desire a deeply spiritual person whose love of God shines through all they do, energizing our parish with faith, purpose and hope.

all_fields.value:
St. Mary’s Episcopal Church embodies the spirit of community and service, uniting people from diverse backgrounds and theological perspectives in a shared commitment to the love of God and one another. With a deep tradition of working together to meet community needs, St. Mary’s lives out its mission of inclusion, acceptance, and service both within the church and beyond.
We seek a priest who embodies spiritual leadership and deep pastoral care, offering unwavering support and guidance throughout all stages of life. Leading with compassion, we hope our next rector can encourage personal growth and stand by our community during both joyful and difficult times, and help us to foster a thriving, resilient faith community. We seek a spiritual leader who can help us build inclusive growth and engagement, continue St. Mary’s tradition of community outreach and service, teach us to live our Christian values in a modern world, and work to support and develop our next generation of leaders.
Please send a cover letter, resume, and OTM portfolio to the Rev. Canon Rob Courtney at rcourtney@episwtn.org.
The Episcopal Diocese of West Tennessee

At Saint Peter’s, Geneva, vibrant faith and creative spirit flourish in a joyful, highly relational parish family rooted in over 150 years of Episcopal heritage. We seek a full-time Rector who brings a true pastoral calling, administrative skill, and visionary insight to our journey in Christ’s love.

Our ideal candidate will be an authentic teacher and Christ-centered preacher; a unifying shepherd who holds traditional and progressive perspectives together, so everyone has a home here; an incarnational leader; and an engaged neighbor.

This role includes leading our dynamic parish, fostering our vibrant music program, and providing direct oversight to the managing director of our 300+ student St. Peter's Community Arts Academy. Our historic campus, recently expanded through a successful capital campaign, offers an inspiring foundation to live with purpose and lead with vision.

Located on the shores of Seneca Lake in the heart of the breathtaking Finger Lakes region, Geneva offers an exceptional quality of life. Residents enjoy world-class wineries, scenic hiking and biking trails, cascading waterfalls, and a vibrant, diverse college-town atmosphere rich in culture, hospitality, and community engagement.

If you are a leader driven by the Holy Spirit, ready to feed our souls and embrace our community as your own, we invite you to join us.

Learn more: stpetersgeneva.org

Interested candidates should send their letter of interest, resume, and OTM/VocationHub Profile to the Rev. Dr. Julianne Buenting, Acting Director of Ministry of the Episcopal Diocese of Rochester at transitions@episcopalrochester.org.

all_fields.value:
St. Paul’s:  Who God is calling us to be…
Through a process of discernment and deep listening, we identified our strengths and weaknesses. We believe that God is calling us to be a welcoming, supportive and safe presence in our community. As an Episcopal community, we have experienced a lot of change over the years and have identified that we need a stronger and more visible presence in our community.
The areas we feel God is calling us to pay attention to are:

-Growing as a congregation spiritually, financially, and in the ways in which we engage the surrounding community.
-Furthering our prayer life through a variety of opportunities: bible study, mid-week Eucharist, and formation. We feel these opportunities are a way for us to deepen our faith and welcome others to community prayer life.
-Exploring opportunities to partner with neighboring Episcopal churches in Turlock and Hanford
Send cover letter, resume, vocations hub profile and 2 video sermons to canonanna@diosanjoaquin.org

all_fields.value:
Who we hope is “Called to be” with us…

St. Paul’s Visalia is hoping that our new Priest-in-Charge will be:

--Administrative:  have strong technology, communication, organizational, and administrative skills, and also be able to delegate responsibilities to our lay leaders

--Collaborative:  be a good listener who values input from the congregation, willing to “get the lay of the land” rather than immediately making changes, interested in reaching out to the community, supportive of our ministry to the unhoused community in our neighborhood, and supportive of the 12-step programs and other groups who call our campus “home”; bilingual (Spanish) and/or Latino/Hispanic cultural competency is a plus

--A person of deep and abiding faith:  this clergy person will need to be able to navigate the complexities of partnership and change while being rooted in their call from God, who is creative about ways to invite more people to church, including families, and they need to be able to share that faith and love with others 

--Loving:  someone who respects diversity at all levels be it race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, ability, age, and socio-economic status, is compassionate, and is committed to providing pastoral care to our parishioners; this person should also be able to interpret scripture to address our lived experiences and current context

all_fields.value: Spiritual leader and pastor of a year-round congregation and a summer chapel. Zion has complete a 3 year priest-in-charge assignment which helped them shift from a 20+ yr rectorship to an empowered laity. Together they addressed a financial deficit, sold the rectory outside of town and invested the proceeds into renovating the rectory near the church and made a significant contribution to their endowment. The congregation has a significant impact in the community and has built relationships with ecumenical partners in the area. Their next priest has an opportunity to build on the current culture of tranparency, inclusion and collaboration.

Canon Linda L Grenz

all_fields.value:
Church of the Resurrection (COR) is the only Episcopal Church in all of Cumberland County, NJ that offers worship services. We are a unique, faithful community in that:

1. Our congregation was formed ten years ago by the merger of 2 congregations, giving up two beautiful buildings, so we could place our emphasis on worshipping together faithfully and on practicing the walk of Jesus by reaching out to our neighbors in need.

2. We are building a new worship space.  Our priest can join us in the decision-making process to design the interior.

3.  During the interim, membership has not declined.  We are a congregation who loves worshiping and serving together!

Our profile is available at https://cumberlandnjepiscopal.org/. Please take a look. We look forward to someone who will guide us to walk in Christ and help us better understand Scripture, while we continue to serve our community together.

The COR is made up of dedicated and enthusiastic Christians anxious to take the next step on their quest to build Cumberland County’s regional Episcopal Church. Much of the hard work has been done. The voluntary merger of St. Andrew’s Bridgeton and Trinity Vineland a decade ago, along with the sale of their costly-to-maintain church buildings sowed the seeds for a financially stable and energized congregation. Recently, parishioners from Christ Church, Millville have joined our congregation. As the only Episcopal Church in Cumberland County to offer worship services, COR is positioned to grow, particularly in light of the fact that so many mainline churches have closed or are in danger of closing. We believe the Episcopal Church has a promising future in our county, and we seek a new leader to live into this new vision with us.

Please contact The Rev. Canon Joan Mason, Transition Ministry Officer 
jmason@dioceseofnj.org or 609-460-5408 
Please submit cover letter, OTM Portfolio, resume, and responses to Clergy Questionnaire found on the diocesan website at: http://dioceseofnj.org/transition-ministry

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value:
Location - We're the only Episcopal Church in Cumberland County that offers worship services.
Adaptability/Resilience - Our parish has survived and prospered while merging two congregations and changes to COR’s worship location.
Operating Costs - By selling its two historic buildings and purchasing its current location, COR dramatically reduced its building maintenance costs.
Welcoming - COR has created a safe and welcoming worship space for the diverse spectrum of Christ’s followers.
Outreach - COR has a strong tradition of community outreach.
Financial Stability - COR is financially sound with a substantial endowment, stable pledges, providing the foundation for success. Total Investments - $1,334,167 (from 2024 Parochial Report)
Expansion - Currently we are adding a new sanctuary and own another tract of land with the ability to build and expand to accommodate growth.

We seek these skills: Energy, Vision, Passionate, Caring, Open minded, Community Oriented.  

We are seeking an engaging speaker who can draw the congregation in and has the ability to bring scripture into our everyday lives, as well as someone who is comfortable working in an inclusive parish. As we progress, we are looking for a forward-thinking leader who enthusiastically embraces outreach to help us grow our church community.  We would like someone who is comfortable working with all age groups who can help grow our Sunday School, re-connect us with inactive members and help rebuild some of the fellowship and traditions lost during the Pandemic.

Most importantly, we are looking for someone who shares our belief in the importance of the Episcopal Church to the universality and inclusivity of Christ’s teachings and the vision to help us create a regional home in Cumberland County for those who share that belief.

all_fields.value: St. Thomas Episcopal Church is searching for a full-time, permanent rector to lead its parishioners through an energetic and spirit-driven expansion which includes leadership over current aging & dedicated congregation and the building of a Hispanic ministry within our own as we have discerned a calling to help our community in this manner.
Resume, Cover Letter addressed to Search Committee, OTM or Vocation Hub profile uploaded to: https://episdionc.formstack.com/forms/applications

all_fields.label: Applicant narrative questions
all_fields.value: 5. What has been your experience leading change in the Church?
Through a grant from the Diocese of NC for salary support, we are able to fund a full-time priest to lead change in establishing an Hispanic congregation within the existing Anglo congregation.

all_fields.label: Details
all_fields.value: The rectory is located within a couple of miles of the church.  It is a 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom home with a den, formal living and dining rooms, sun room, kitchen, basement, garage and carport.  Estimated square footage is 1800 sq feet.  There is an expectation the full-time rector will live at the Rectory.

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value: The new priest should be passionate about Jesus and His teachings.  We want our new priest to be a positive person, one who can motivate and uplift the current congregation to accomplish goals of spiritual and physical growth.  We want our priest to be kind and compassionate to all people and set the example for loving each and every person.  We ask that he/she to be patient with us and our congregation as we learn and grow together.  We require honesty and dependability.  We prefer someone who is hard-working, not afraid to “dig in” and work with the congregation.  We need a critical thinker, one who can help us solve the problems of today and tomorrow.    We are hoping for flexibility and understanding, but a boldness to stand with us as we grow our church.

Christ Church is a place where all are welcome. We are a grand mixture, diverse in occupation, sexual orientation, socio-economic level, political orientation, and theology. This facilitates our being nimble and able to live out our baptismal ministries in many ways, open to new possibilities. In that spirit we have been addressing our biggest challenge: our buildings. In 2015 a crane collapsed onto the roof and we have since suffered two floods which have seriously impacted our church and parish hall. We have explored a variety of options including partnering with another Episcopal church and having conversations with other denominational churches (most facing the same issues) about a possible new shared building. We also are working with a firm to assess the renovation and waterproofing options as well as potential development options for our property. We are in the heart of our capital city and are known for our extensive outreach ministries, inspiring worship and ministry with children and youth. We have been supported in our discernment process by a former canon of the diocese and now are seeking a priest with an adventurous spirit and who is willing to lead us into a new future. We have a strong lay leadership core and our congregation has been engaged in facing the reality of our situation. We see this as an opportunity to step out of the boat and follow Jesus to wherever he leads us.

Email resume, letter and OTM to: Transition@diovermont.org
The Rev. Canon Linda L. Grenz, Canon for Transitions

There is a mission support grant for $30,000 to help with the operational expenses of the congregation.  There are three additional congregations that rent space from St Francis to help with operational expenses--an Armenian church, an ELCA church (shared worship with St Francis) and a non-denominational church.

all_fields.label: Sabbatical Provision Details
all_fields.value: Yes; 3 months of paid sabbatical leave after 6 years of continual service

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value:
The areas we feel God is calling us to pay attention to are: 
-Growing as a congregation spiritually, financially, and in the ways in which we engage the surrounding community. 
-Identifying our gifts and skills, and inviting others from the Turlock community into mission and ministry -Furthering our prayer life through a variety of opportunities: bible study, mid-week Eucharist, and formation. We feel these opportunities are a way for us to deepen our faith and welcome others to community prayer life. -Developing a relationship with UC Stanislaus students, faculty and staff -Growing and developing our food ministry 
-Growing into a leader of the Northern Deanery. As our community grows in cohesion and leadership, we can expand our community presence. 

St. Francis Turlock is hoping that our new Priest-in-Charge will be:
--Entrepreneurial:  have big dreams and big ideas for how we can co-create the kingdom of God in the city of Turlock; a priest who is inspiring, creative, energetic and enthusiastic in their preaching and leadership, as well as having some financial management experience 
--Collaborative:  willing and able to work with our partners like Light of Christ ELCA, and develop partnerships throughout the Turlock community; we also believe our Priest-in-Charge should be willing and able to work collaboratively with the Bishop’s Committee 
--A person of deep and abiding faith:  this clergy person will need to be able to navigate the complexities of partnership and change while being rooted in their call from God, and they need to be able to share that faith and love with others  
--Loving:  someone who respects diversity at all levels be it race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, ability, age, and socio-economic status, and is committed to providing pastoral care to our parishioners

all_fields.label: Congregation
all_fields.value: St John the Evangelist Epis Church

St John’s is located in downtown Stockton, is visible and accessible to the community. Our vision is to embody Christ’s authentic hospitality, as we see our church where every person is welcomed at the table, fully belonging and deeply known. Our dream is to have a clergy person who will take on the liturgical and pastoral responsibility of our church, stimulate our growth, lead us by example, with discernment, consistency, inclusivity, compassion and courage. It is the hope of the Bishop and Diocesan Council that under the guidance of the new Priest-in-Charge, St. John’s will continue to grow our congregation, remain fervent to our ministries, serve our community, and help us move from Mission to Parish status. 

Who we hope is “Called to be” with us… 
--Collaborative: must be “hands on” with our community and our partners such as Uplift All Foundation, and be present when services such as showers, laundry, meals, medical checks, etc are being provided 
--Welcoming: St John’s is a place for everyone, a sanctuary for those struggling with mental health and addiction, the unhoused and forgotten; we are guided by the Spirit to love and serve all 
--Community focused: our message of “love and care for all” travels throughout the city. We are present at Pride events in June and October, we participate in the Run and Walk for the Emergency Food Bank, we aid the work of the Mercy Peddlers, we host fundraising events 
---Entrepreneurial: have big dreams and big ideas for how we can develop our ministries in the heart of downtown Stockton, a priest who is inspiring, creative, energetic and enthusiastic, as well as having financial management experience; we aspire to explore Latino ministry in our context. Our urban location is bisected by the Cinco de Mayo and Our Lady of Guadalupe events. 
--Faithful: this clergy person will need to be able to navigate the complexities of partnership and change while being rooted in their call from God, and they need to be able to share that faith and love with others
Send cover letter, resume, and vocations hub profile along with two video sermons to canonanna@diosanjoaquin.org

How do you incorporate accessibility and inclusion in your ministry?, 13. What is a ministry risk you've taken, and what came of it?, 14. How do you discern and adapt to the changing needs of your community?, 16. In what ways are you engaged in your wider Church or local community?

St John's has a trust which helps to support the upkeep of the buildings and grounds of the church.  It also has several long-term rentals on the property which support the operational budget, as well as using the parish hall for private parties and meetings.

all_fields.label: This is a position serving multiple congregations or organizations.
all_fields.value:
Church of Jesus (Rosebud)
Family Worship (Norris)
Grace (Soldier Creek)
Holy Innocents (Parmelee)
St Thomas (Corn Creek)
Sts Philip and James (White River)
Trinity (Mission)

This position is on the Rosebud Indian Reservation in south-central South Dakota. These congregations are small in *attendance,* but the Rosebud Episcopal Mission is huge in impact and reaching the people of the Rosebud. Serving here means serving in one of the three poorest counties in the United States, so poverty -- and responding to it faithfully -- is a major part of life on the Rosebud. As Superintending Presbyter, you will be in charge of these congregations, but worry not: They do not all worship on Sundays! Three do: Church of Jesus, Rosebud; Trinity, Mission; and Holy Innocents, Parmelee. The latter is in the final stages of being rebuilt after burning down due to arson. The other congregations worship on a sporadic basis, sometimes once a month, sometimes two to five times a year. 
A major portion of your time on the Rosebud will be focused on wakes and funerals, not just for members of the congregations, but for community members who have no church affiliation, or have not been to church in years. The Rosebud Episcopal Mission is recognized and valued for caring for all of the people of the Rosebud, especially in their times of great need. Forty or more funerals, each with two days of wakes, consume from 50 percent to 65 percent of your time. The funerals are not one-hour services, but can take from four to 10 hours.
The ideal candidate will be someone who is open and willing to learn about and respect the Lakota culture and history, and will learn how to work together with Lakota spiritual leaders without cultural apprehension. Speaking the Lakota language is not necessary, but being willing to learn how to sing in Lakota is essential. (Worry not: The people will teach you!)

Please view the profile movies and written profile at https://www.episcopalchurchsd.org/page/rosebud. Send a letter of application, as well as your current resume and current candidate profile, as well as the answer to the question below, to The Rev. Dr. Lauren R. Stanley, Canon to the Ordinary, lauren@episcopalchurchsd.org. For more information, contact Canon Stanley at 605-494-2020.

In what ways have you grown in cultural competency as a leader?, 10. What theological convictions shape your ministry?, 15. How do you take care of your well-being-spiritually, emotionally, and physically?
The Rev. Dr. Lauren R. Stanley

all_fields.label: Details
all_fields.value: Salary is dependent upon years of experience. The range actually is $38,000 - $50,000, unless the candidate has more than 20 years of experience. This position is part of the grant that the Diocese of South Dakota receives from The General Convention for our work with the Indigenous population.

all_fields.label: Details
all_fields.value: Three-bedroom, 1.5-bath, with a fireplace, central air and central heating (propane), with a fenced yard.

all_fields.label: Details
all_fields.value:
Dental: Yes (members pay 100 percent of the monthly premium)
Health Care: Each clergy person pays 15 percent of the monthly bill for the insurance. (That is taken out of their paychecks automatically.)

all_fields.label: Vacation & Leave Details
all_fields.value:
Five weeks, including five Sundays
15 National Holidays

all_fields.label: Continuing Education Details
all_fields.value: 2 weeks, along with $1,000 yearly. Clergy are required to do continuing education every year, and file a report on that education yearly.

all_fields.label: Sabbatical Provision Details
all_fields.value: Yes: Three months after five years of service.

all_fields.label: Travel/Auto Account Details
all_fields.value: Mileage provided at the current IRS rate.

all_fields.label: Equipment/Phone Details
all_fields.value: The Mission Council is encouraged to pay for the cellular service only of the clergy person. (All other portions of the cellular bill are the responsibility of the clergy.)

all_fields.label: Other Benefits or Comments
all_fields.value:
South Dakota has a great Bishop and clergy who will be supportive and caring. The priest who takes this position will have two mentors, one from the Pine Ridge Episcopal Mission, who has great experience on the reservation just to the west of the Rosebud. The other mentor will be the Canon to the Ordinary, who served on the Rosebud for 8.5 years.

This position is not for the faint of heart. It requires a love for all of God's beloved children, an ability to be flexible every single day, a willingness to learn about and respect the Lakota culture and history, and ability to build your own support system off the reservation.

The Rosebud Episcopal Mission was created in 1875 at the request of Lakota leader Sinte Gleska (Spotted Tail), who requested an Episcopal priest, an Episcopal teacher, and a translator.

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value:
More than anything, the people of the Rosebud are praying for a priest who will love them, no matter what. They pray for a person who will be out in the community, who will learn about and respect the Lakota way of life, especially from the Sicangu (Burnt Thigh Nation) perspective. They pray for a person who will be present, who will NOT appropriate the culture to themselves but who will defend that culture. 

The ideal candidate is someone who has lived in the context of poverty (such as a Peace Corps volunteer, or a person who has been on mission visits to other countries). Because the Rosebud is one of the poorest counties in the nation (always in the top three, alas), the priest who comes to the Rosebud will need to understand how a community functions while dealing with the extreme poverty that is a part of everyday life. We are not asking the priest to be poor; we are seeking a priest who understands that helping each other and caring for each other is a core component of this ministry. We do not want a priest who constantly laments, "Why can't people just ...." We seek a priest who says, "I understand ..."

We are also seeking a priest who has a tremendous amount of patience, especially when it comes to building trust. The Lakota people have seen clergy come and go, and will wait and watch for a period of time before investing themselves in the latest person to come along. Respecting that waiting and watching, and working to build trust by showing up, and by supporting people, is a key component of this work.

In addition, because this is a multipoint position, it is imperative to NOT pick favorites among the congregations. Some of these congregations only worship two to six times per year. That doesn't mean they are not as worthy as those that worship weekly. (This falls under the category of "Different is not better, it is not worse, it is simply 'different.'") The priest may not hold worship services is each community weekly, but they will be in those communities for wakes, funerals, memorials, and celebrations. SHOWING UP is another key component.

There are some programs that are ongoing, such as the #FirewoodfortheElders program, which provides free firewood to those in need and is run by lay people who know what they are doing. The priest will help coordinate that work, and will need to do fundraising to support it (it is not part of the budget). The youth program is in the process of being re-created, again led by a lay person.

all_fields.value: Located on opposite sides of the CT river, these two congregations have laid the groundwork for together welcoming a priest for a FT call. St. James is located in downtown Glastonbury with a nice physical plant and well-positioned to engaged in ministry to the community. Trinity is located in Old Wethersfield in a lovely historic building. They are excited for what the future could hold for them.
Apply on https://www.episcopalct.org/open-positions/clergy-employment/ and look for St. James Glastonbury and Trinity Wethersfield.

all_fields.value: Our commitment to you, our new associate rector, is to cultivate an environment that encourages you to grow, deepen, and lean confidently into your unique gifts of ministry, supported by collaborative leadership and space for faithful innovation.  Working closely with the Rector, staff, and lay leaders, you will share in the full sacramental and pastoral life of our vibrant and inclusive parish through intergenerational ministries that includes oversight of our children and youth ministries.
To apply for this position, contact Anne Graner, Search Chair - search@stpaulsnewnan.org
St. Paul's Episcopal Church, Newnan, GA

all_fields.label: Details
all_fields.value: Compensation for the Associate Rector position is $69,972. Total compensation includes SECA, which is $4972.

all_fields.label: Continuing Education Details
all_fields.value: Two weeks of Continuing Education. Budget: up to $1,000 per year

all_fields.label: Sabbatical Provision Details
all_fields.value: Sabbatical eligibility after five to eight years of ordained ministry

all_fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
all_fields.value:
Our search committee held multiple listening sessions with our parishioners. We received valuable input that we synthesized into four attributes we desire in our candidates for Associate
Rector:

Strategic Catalyst for Dynamic Ministries
* Discovers parishioners’ talents and gifts and inspires them to use their gifts to grow St. Paul’s and its
impact on each other and the community.
* Is willing to experiment and learn by developing intergenerational and other creative programming.

Engaging Relationship Developer
* Passionately builds relationships with children, youth, and families, cultivating connection with our
diocese, the wider community, and the life of the church through shared ministries.
* Values diversity and adapts to different settings and people with pastoral sensitivity.
* Brings a healthy sense of humor to ministry.

Effective Collaborative Leader
* Seeks collaboration with parishioners and with other clergy and staff, fostering a culture of belonging.
* Is intrinsically motivated and organized. Values accountability and quality communication.

Inspirational Spiritual Guide
* Helps create and oversee compelling children and youth formation by balancing biblical knowledge and spiritual growth programming with engaging activities at church and in the community.
* Impacts parishioners through sermons, reflections, spiritual practices, discussions and activities.

all_fields.label: Leadership skills (maximum of 5)
all_fields.value: Appreciative Inquiry, Collaborative Leadership, Congregational Development, Strategic Planning, Quality Communication

all_fields.label: Ministry skills  (maximum of 10)
all_fields.value: Formation, Intergenerational Formation, Pastoral Care, Prayer & Spirituality, Laity Empowerment

The Curate will work closely with the Rector to lead, manage, and facilitate the parish's liturgical, pastoral, and spiritual life.  The Curate will oversee specific ministries as delegated by the Rector while assisting with preaching, services, staff management, and other duties as assigned.
To apply for the Curate position, contact The Rev. Dr. Bill Murray - bmurray@holyinnocents.org.

vacation: Standard vacation time, Continuing Education of two weeks, seven days of spiritual retreat time.

all_fields.value:
Grace Episcopal Church seeks a part-time priest who is warm, caring and enjoys a life of service.  A priest who would like to help others develop their spiritual gifts and offers thought-provoking sermons. A priest who will lead with a sense of fun while being a creative problem solver.  
The leadership of Grace is open to receiving a priest that would like a flexible work schedule.
Kindly submit your materials, cover letter, resume and Vocation Hub profile or OTM pdf during this transition time to the Rev. Canon Anita Braden.

all_fields.label: Applicant narrative questions
all_fields.value: 1. How do you approach pastoral care in your ministry?, 3. What does stewardship mean to you, and how do you practice it?, 12. Describe a recent ministry experiment or innovation.
